title: synostemon crassifolius
intro: Synostemon crassifolius, also known as the rough-leaved synostemon, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its thick, leathery leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Synostemon crassifolium include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. Synostemon crassifolium can be prepared as an infusion or a topical ointment, and is also used in some traditional remedies as a treatment for snake bites.
